name: F-16 Fighting Falcon

identification.manufacturer: General Dynamics design (now Lockheed Martin); single-engine, bubble canopy with no centerline frame.
identification.family: Single-engine multirole fighter — light, agile, with a chin-mounted air intake and a frameless bubble canopy.
identification.variant: F-16 baseline jet — most-produced fighter of its generation.

keyTell: Single chin intake, frameless bubble canopy, single tailfin.

name: F-22 Raptor

identification.manufacturer: Lockheed Martin stealth lineage shared with F-117/F-35.
identification.family: 5th-generation air-superiority fighter — twin-engine, twin-tail, faceted stealth fuselage.
identification.variant: F-22A — only operational variant, retired from production.

keyTell: Twin canted tails, diamond-shape wing planform, internal weapons bays.

name: F-35 Lightning II

identification.manufacturer: Lockheed Martin's family of stealth multirole fighters built around a single F135 engine.
identification.family: 5th-generation single-engine stealth strike fighter; chunky fuselage to fit the lift fan / fuel.
identification.variant: Common F-35 silhouette across A/B/C variants; B has the lift-fan door behind the cockpit.

keyTell: Single engine, fat fuselage, twin tails, chined nose.

name: F/A-18 Hornet

identification.manufacturer: McDonnell Douglas design now sustained by Boeing — twin-engine carrier fighter.
identification.family: Carrier-based multirole fighter: twin canted tails, leading-edge root extensions running to the canopy.
identification.variant: Legacy Hornet (A/B/C/D); Super Hornet is a separate, larger airframe.

keyTell: Twin canted tails, prominent LERX, dual underwing intakes.

name: A-10 Thunderbolt II

identification.manufacturer: Fairchild Republic — purpose-built around the GAU-8 Avenger 30 mm cannon.
identification.family: Close-air-support attack jet, straight wings, twin engines mounted high at the rear.
identification.variant: Single A-10 design (A-10A baseline, A-10C upgrade).

keyTell: Straight wings, rear-mounted engines, twin tails, nose cannon.

name: B-52 Stratofortress

identification.manufacturer: Boeing — long-running strategic bomber from the 1950s, still in service.
identification.family: Eight-engine swept-wing strategic bomber, tall single tail.
identification.variant: B-52H — only operational variant.

keyTell: Eight engines in four paired pods, drooping wings, tall single tail.

name: B-2 Spirit

identification.manufacturer: Northrop Grumman — flying-wing stealth lineage from the YB-49.
identification.family: Stealth strategic bomber, flying-wing planform, no vertical tail.
identification.variant: Single B-2A design.

keyTell: All-wing stealth bomber, no tail, sawtooth trailing edge.

name: C-130 Hercules

identification.manufacturer: Lockheed (now Lockheed Martin) — defining tactical airlifter since the 1950s.
identification.family: Four-engine high-wing turboprop tactical transport with rear loading ramp.
identification.variant: Many sub-variants (E/H/J); J-model has six-blade scimitar props.

keyTell: High-wing turboprop, four props, rear ramp, slab-side fuselage.

name: C-17 Globemaster III

identification.manufacturer: McDonnell Douglas design now built and sustained by Boeing.
identification.family: Four-engine high-wing strategic airlifter, T-tail, large winglets.
identification.variant: Single C-17A design.

keyTell: Big T-tail, four high-bypass turbofans, prominent blended winglets.

name: AH-64 Apache

identification.manufacturer: Hughes/McDonnell Douglas/Boeing lineage — Apache first flew 1975.
identification.family: Tandem-seat attack helicopter, four-blade main rotor, stub-wing weapons pylons.
identification.variant: AH-64D Longbow has the mast-mounted radar dome; AH-64E is the latest.

keyTell: Tandem cockpit, chin-mounted gun, stub wings with weapons pylons.

name: UH-60 Black Hawk

identification.manufacturer: Sikorsky — utility helicopter family that replaced the UH-1 Huey.
identification.family: Twin-engine medium utility helicopter, four-blade main rotor, stabilator at the tail.
identification.variant: UH-60 baseline; HH-60 search-and-rescue, MH-60 special ops, etc.

keyTell: Squat fuselage, sliding cabin doors, large stabilator on tail boom.

name: MQ-9 Reaper

identification.manufacturer: General Atomics — successor to the MQ-1 Predator.
identification.family: Long-endurance armed UAV, V-tail, single rear-mounted turboprop pusher.
identification.variant: MQ-9A baseline; MQ-9B SkyGuardian variant for civil airspace.

keyTell: Pusher prop on the tail, V-tail, bulged sensor nose.

name: Su-27 Flanker

identification.manufacturer: Sukhoi — basis for an entire family of derivatives (Su-30/33/34/35).
identification.family: Long-fuselage twin-engine air-superiority fighter, leading-edge root extension, twin tails.
identification.variant: Baseline Su-27 / Su-27S; many evolved variants follow.

keyTell: Long sleek fuselage, twin engines spaced apart with a sting between, twin tails.

name: MiG-29 Fulcrum

identification.manufacturer: Mikoyan-Gurevich — front-line tactical fighter contemporary of the Su-27.
identification.family: Twin-engine tactical fighter, leading-edge root extensions, intakes with blow-in doors for grass-strip ops.
identification.variant: Many sub-variants; later versions add helmet-mounted sights and AESA upgrades.

keyTell: Twin engines close together, twin canted tails, large LERX, smaller than a Flanker.

name: Eurofighter Typhoon

identification.manufacturer: Multinational program (UK / Germany / Italy / Spain) under the Eurofighter consortium.
identification.family: Delta-canard 4.5-generation fighter, twin engines, single fin.
identification.variant: Tranche 1/2/3 with progressively newer avionics and radar (CAPTOR-E AESA).

keyTell: Cropped delta wing with large forward canards, chin intake, single tail.

name: Dassault Rafale

identification.manufacturer: Dassault — France's omnirole 4.5-generation fighter, sized between Mirage 2000 and Typhoon.
identification.family: Delta-canard fighter with twin engines and oval intakes mounted under the canard root.
identification.variant: C (single-seat AdA) / B (two-seat) / M (carrier).

keyTell: Smaller than Typhoon, oval intakes under the canards, twin engines close-coupled.

name: Saab JAS 39 Gripen

identification.manufacturer: Saab — Sweden's lightweight delta-canard multirole fighter.
identification.family: Single-engine delta-canard, designed for short austere runways.
identification.variant: C/D legacy and the larger E/F (Gripen E).

keyTell: Single engine, small delta-canard, looks compact and light next to Rafale/Typhoon.

name: F-14 Tomcat

identification.manufacturer: Grumman — the iconic carrier interceptor of the late Cold War.
identification.family: Twin-engine variable-sweep wing fleet defender, two-seat tandem cockpit.
identification.variant: F-14A/B/D — retired from US service in 2006.

keyTell: Variable-sweep wings, twin tails, widely-spaced engine nacelles, two-seat cockpit.

name: F-15C Eagle

identification.manufacturer: McDonnell Douglas (now Boeing) air-superiority Eagle.
identification.family: Large twin-engine air-superiority fighter, twin tails, big intakes.
identification.variant: F-15C single-seat air-to-air variant — no conformal fuel tanks, lighter than the Strike Eagle.

keyTell: Big twin tails, no CFTs on the fuselage, single-seat canopy.

name: F-15E Strike Eagle

identification.manufacturer: McDonnell Douglas / Boeing — strike-optimized Eagle derivative.
identification.family: Two-seat all-weather strike fighter built on the F-15 airframe.
identification.variant: F-15E adds conformal fuel tanks and a back-seat WSO; F-15EX is a newer derivative.

keyTell: Two-seat tandem canopy plus conformal fuel tanks down the fuselage flanks.

name: MiG-21 Fishbed

identification.manufacturer: Mikoyan-Gurevich — most-produced supersonic jet of all time.
identification.family: Single-engine tailed-delta interceptor, nose intake with shock cone.
identification.variant: Many variants; late versions have larger spine and broader nose.

keyTell: Tiny tailed-delta with circular nose intake and shock cone.

name: MiG-23 Flogger

identification.manufacturer: Mikoyan-Gurevich — variable-geometry follow-on to the MiG-21.
identification.family: Single-engine variable-sweep fighter with side intakes (unlike the MiG-21's nose intake).
identification.variant: MS/MF/ML for fighter, MiG-27 is the strike-optimized derivative.

keyTell: Variable-sweep wings on a slab-sided fuselage with rectangular side intakes.

name: Sukhoi Su-30

identification.manufacturer: Sukhoi — multirole twin-seat development of the Su-27.
identification.family: Two-seat tandem Flanker derivative; Su-30MKI/SM versions add canards and thrust-vectoring.
identification.variant: MKI (India) and SM (Russia) are the most common.

keyTell: Flanker silhouette but with a tandem two-seat canopy; some variants have canards.

name: Sukhoi Su-35

identification.manufacturer: Sukhoi — single-seat 4.5-gen Flanker with thrust-vectoring AL-41F1S engines.
identification.family: Heavily refined Flanker with no canards but new larger tail and reinforced airframe.
identification.variant: Su-35S is the production variant.

keyTell: Single-seat Flanker silhouette, no canards, very tall pitot tube on the nose.

name: Panavia Tornado IDS

identification.manufacturer: Panavia — joint UK / Germany / Italy program for a low-level strike aircraft.
identification.family: Two-seat variable-sweep all-weather strike jet, twin engines under a slab fuselage.
identification.variant: IDS strike, ECR SEAD, ADV (UK air-defence variant with longer fuselage).

keyTell: Variable-sweep wings, two-seat tandem canopy, single tall fin, low-set engines.

name: Dassault Mirage 2000

identification.manufacturer: Dassault — pure delta-wing fighter without canards (unlike the Rafale).
identification.family: Single-engine tailless delta with a pronounced cropped delta planform.
identification.variant: C/D/N/-5/-9 spanning interception, strike, and nuclear-strike roles.

keyTell: Pure delta wing, no canards, single engine, single tail — looks like a flatter, simpler design than the Rafale.

name: F-4 Phantom II

identification.manufacturer: McDonnell Douglas — Cold War twin-engine multirole that served US and many allies.
identification.family: Large twin-engine two-seat fighter with anhedral horizontal stabilator and dihedral outer wing panels.
identification.variant: F-4B / E / G / J — many subvariants across services.

keyTell: Drooping horizontal stabilator, upward-cocked outer wings, two-seat tandem cockpit.

name: F-5 Tiger II

identification.manufacturer: Northrop — small twin-engine lightweight fighter exported widely.
identification.family: Compact supersonic fighter with sharp pointed nose and long LERX.
identification.variant: F-5E Tiger II is the most common; F-5F is the two-seat trainer.

keyTell: Tiny twin-engine fighter with pointy nose and prominent LERX — much smaller than an F-15.

name: Chengdu J-20

identification.manufacturer: Chengdu Aircraft Corporation — China's first 5th-generation stealth fighter.
identification.family: Twin-engine stealth fighter with a long fuselage, canards, and delta wings.
identification.variant: J-20A baseline; J-20B with thrust-vectoring engines is the latest.

keyTell: Long stealth airframe with both canards and a delta wing — the only operational stealth fighter with canards.

name: Tu-95 Bear

identification.manufacturer: Tupolev — Soviet strategic bomber, the only swept-wing turboprop bomber in service.
identification.family: Four-engine swept-wing strategic bomber with contra-rotating turboprops.
identification.variant: Tu-95MS is the modern cruise-missile carrier variant.

keyTell: Four turboprops with massive contra-rotating propellers — the only swept-wing turboprop bomber.

name: Airbus A400M Atlas

identification.manufacturer: Airbus Defence & Space — Europe's tactical/strategic airlifter program.
identification.family: Four-engine high-wing turboprop tactical transport, between the C-130 and the C-17 in size.
identification.variant: Single A400M design across operators.

keyTell: Four eight-blade scimitar-style turboprops; props on inboard and outboard engines turn opposite directions.

name: V-22 Osprey

identification.manufacturer: Bell Helicopter and Boeing joint program — first operational tiltrotor.
identification.family: Tiltrotor that takes off like a helicopter and cruises like a turboprop.
identification.variant: MV-22 (USMC), CV-22 (USAF special ops), HV-22 (search & rescue).

keyTell: Wings tip with massive proprotor nacelles that swivel between vertical and horizontal.

name: CH-47 Chinook

identification.manufacturer: Boeing — heavy-lift tandem-rotor helicopter in service since the 1960s.
identification.family: Twin tandem-rotor heavy-lift helicopter, no tail rotor.
identification.variant: CH-47D / F (Army), MH-47 special ops; F-model is the current production variant.

keyTell: Two large overlapping rotors front and rear — no tail rotor at all.

name: F-117 Nighthawk

identification.manufacturer: Lockheed Skunk Works — first operational stealth attack jet.
identification.family: Faceted-stealth subsonic strike aircraft, V-tail, twin engines buried in the fuselage.
identification.variant: F-117A — retired in 2008, occasionally flown for research.

keyTell: Highly faceted black airframe with a V-tail and screened intakes — classic 'stealth' look.

name: B-1B Lancer

identification.manufacturer: Rockwell International (now part of Boeing) — strategic supersonic bomber.
identification.family: Variable-sweep four-engine heavy bomber.
identification.variant: B-1B — only operational variant; A-model was a different program.

keyTell: Sleek blended fuselage with variable-sweep wings; engines paired in two underwing pods.

name: E-3 Sentry (AWACS)

identification.manufacturer: Boeing — based on the 707 airframe, the iconic AWACS platform.
identification.family: Four-engine airborne early-warning aircraft with a rotating radar dome on top.
identification.variant: E-3A/B/C/D/F — used by USAF, NATO, UK, France, Saudi Arabia.

keyTell: Rotating radar disc mounted on twin pylons above a 707-style fuselage.

name: KC-135 Stratotanker

identification.manufacturer: Boeing — the original four-engine USAF tanker, ancestor of the 707.
identification.family: Four-engine swept-wing aerial refueling tanker with a flying boom under the tail.
identification.variant: KC-135R is the re-engined modern fleet.

keyTell: Looks like a Boeing 707 but with a flying boom hanging from the rear fuselage.

name: Mi-24 Hind

identification.manufacturer: Mil Moscow Helicopter Plant — Soviet attack/transport hybrid.
identification.family: Heavy attack helicopter that also carries up to eight troops; stub wings with weapons pylons.
identification.variant: Mi-24/35 series; tandem cockpit and bubble canopies on later variants.

keyTell: Tandem stepped cockpit with bubble canopies, stub wings with rocket pods, troop cabin behind.

name: AV-8B Harrier II

identification.manufacturer: McDonnell Douglas / British Aerospace — second-generation Harrier.
identification.family: Single-engine subsonic V/STOL attack aircraft with vectored thrust.
identification.variant: AV-8B (USMC) and Harrier GR.7/9 (UK, retired).

keyTell: Single Pegasus engine with four rotating exhaust nozzles low on the fuselage; high-mounted swept wing.

name: Sukhoi Su-57 Felon

identification.manufacturer: Sukhoi — Russia's first operational 5th-generation stealth fighter.
identification.family: Twin-engine 5th-generation stealth fighter with widely-spaced engines (LERX-style fuselage).
identification.variant: Su-57 baseline; production has been slow, fielded in limited numbers.

keyTell: Looks like a stealthier Flanker — twin engines spaced far apart, faceted nose, twin canted tails.

name: Tu-160 Blackjack

identification.manufacturer: Tupolev — Soviet/Russian variable-geometry strategic bomber.
identification.family: Largest, heaviest variable-sweep aircraft ever built; visually similar to a B-1B but bigger and white.
identification.variant: Tu-160M is the modernized Russian variant.

keyTell: Like a B-1B in shape but larger, painted bright white, with engines in widely separated nacelles.

name: T-38 Talon

identification.manufacturer: Northrop — twin-engine supersonic jet trainer derived from the F-5 lineage.
identification.family: Compact twin-engine tandem trainer, sharp pointed nose and slim fuselage.
identification.variant: T-38A is the standard USAF trainer; AT-38 has gun and pylons for weapons training.

keyTell: Looks like a slimmer F-5 with a tandem cockpit — twin engines, pointed nose, no wing pylons by default.
